refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify compliance with the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement robust error correction and retransmission protocols.
DP2: Use adaptive bitrate and compression algorithms to optimize data flow.
DP3: Prioritize teleoperation data packets over less critical data.
DP4: Real-time network quality monitoring and dynamic adjustment.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement robust error correction and retransmission protocols.

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically addresses the FR independently and does not introduce coupling with unrelated functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk that error correction overhead may increase latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating adaptive bitrate (DP2) if packet loss remains high despite error correction.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software module responsible for teleoperation communication management and control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to report communication quality metrics including packet loss.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands and task instructions.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope; contains teleoperation but also other functions, leading to moderate diffusion.
Primarily physical control; less suitable for communication protocol implementation.
Highest containment score; focused on teleoperation communication, minimal diffusion.
Focused on safety alerts, not communication quality.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality management, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Network Link interface stability.
Risk of insufficient integration with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure clear interface contracts with Safety Services for safety-related communication.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with clear verification methods and manageable assumptions.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as DP is contained within Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best IM candidate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and network testing within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Review integration points with Safety Services to ensure safety-critical communication is not compromised.
Monitor latency impact of error correction protocols.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition level is complete with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment meeting containment and interface criteria. Verification and risk considerations are addressed sufficiently for this level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

revise.why: Current level is consistent and complete; no immediate revisions required.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Error correction protocols do not introduce unacceptable latency.
open_risks: Potential integration challenges with Safety Services.
evidence_needed: Empirical packet loss measurements under operational conditions.
Latency impact analysis of error correction implementation.
